translated: It was around the scene where MIKU-chan asks Shizuku-chan how she's doing.
In the scene where they watch footage of Shizuku from her Cheerful Days era, there are lines from the past Shizuku.
"I need to do it more like this..."
We actually recorded several different patterns of her muttering to herself like that.
Really.
We delivered one of those versions to all of you, but...
For the patterns, there was a version of Shizuku that felt more precarious, like you'd be worried just watching her.
She was really pushing herself to the limit, thinking she had to master the dance to carry the weight of being the center of Cheerful Days.
I recorded several takes of that desperate Shizuku. I felt like...
We've seen Shizuku backed into a corner like this a few times before,
but it's been a while since she went that far. It was actually quite tough,
and there were several scenes that were pretty painful to perform.
Flashbacks to the Cheerful Days era always involve lines where she's pushing herself, which can be hard for me too.
But that Shizuku is what leads to the Shizuku we have now.
I thought having that contrast would create more of a story.
That's what I felt.
And yet, a cornered Shizuku has a certain charm of her own,
in a way.
Especially since she was being promoted as the "mysterious Shizuku" back then,
she never showed that side of her hard work.
That's why seeing her silently working so hard by herself behind the scenes feels so lonely.
Like she was doing her absolute best all on her own.
That's why it connects to the episode with her juniors I mentioned earlier,
where Shizuku feels like she has to work hard
for their sake as well.
Thinking about it that way, I'm so glad she met Momojan.
Seriously, Minori...
Haruka...
